mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate directly raises the discount rate for equity valuations, mechanically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate hike months are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that override cyclical earnings growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in consumer prices and the federal funds rate create a stagflationary pricing environment that compresses equity multiples, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint increases in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS yield positive 20d returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in the index of industrial production signals strengthening supply-side capacity that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5d returns with a Rank IC > 0.02 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales of automobiles and parts reflects resilient consumer discretionary spending, which directly supports corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20d forward returns across 5-year rolling samples.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 240, inflationary pressures force central banks to prioritize price stability over growth, triggering aggressive policy tightening that suppresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60d returns in >70% of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 240.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and a stable or declining unemployment rate indicate a robust labor market that sustains wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 20d returns in >60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts reflects tightening mortgage credit conditions that spill over into broader financial markets, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines are followed by positive 60d returns in >65% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%.
